title: Local Clusters, Cluster Lifecycle, Conformance, Upgrades, Backup, and Production Defense · API Object Lens: K8s Kubeadm Controlplane Etcd
summary: An operations board must distinguish a local minikube proof from production, upgrade a supported cluster without skipped minors, recover control-plane state, and defend remaining risks. Planned artifact: Add and verify the k8s kubeadm controlplane etcd increment in cluster lifecycle and recovery defense. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace kubeadm bootstrap, certificates, static Pods, control-plane endpoints, etcd membership, kubelet configuration, CNI installation, join tokens, and high-availability responsibilities.
Create and inspect a minikube 1.38.1 learning cluster while naming driver, node, runtime, networking, storage, add-on, tunnel, resource, and production-transfer limits.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Predict K8s Kubeadm Controlplane Etcd · Theory K8s Kubeadm Controlplane Etcd
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory board plans a 1.35 to 1.36 control-plane upgrade with skewed kubelets, deprecated APIs, expired recovery assumptions, untested etcd restore, and unclear rollback. Defend compatibility, drain, backup, staged upgrade, recovery, and ownership. Competency probe: Trace kubeadm bootstrap, certificates, static Pods, control-plane endpoints, etcd membership, kubelet configuration, CNI installation, join tokens, and high-availability responsibilities.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example board plans a 1.35 to 1.36 control-plane upgrade with skewed kubelets, deprecated APIs, expired recovery assumptions, untested etcd restore, and unclear rollback. Defend compatibility, drain, backup, staged upgrade, recovery, and ownership. Competency probe: Trace kubeadm bootstrap, certificates, static Pods, control-plane endpoints, etcd membership, kubelet configuration, CNI installation, join tokens, and high-availability responsibilities. A defensible solution applies K8s Kubeadm Controlplane Etcd, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to cluster lifecycle and recovery defense.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-worked-k8s-cluster-lifecycle-upgrade-recovery-3
  - competency: k8s-worked-k8s-cluster-lifecycle-upgrade-recovery-3
    artifact: cluster-recovery-defense.yaml
    decision: worked k8s cluster lifecycle upgrade recovery 3
    environment: shared multi-tenant cluster under Restricted Pod Security
    fault-injection: drop one metrics series during an autoscaling surge
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: worked3
    change: |
      baseline: kubernetes-1.36.2
      local-proof: minikube-1.38.1
      distribution: supported-and-conformant
      upgrade: patch-current-then-next-minor
      order: api-server-then-controllers-then-kubelet
      drain: capacity-and-pdb-verified
      etcd: encrypted-snapshot-restored
      application-state: independently-restored
      rto-rpo: rehearsed
    verify: kubectl get nodes -o json && kubeadm upgrade plan && etcdctl snapshot status backup.db
    failure: Reject minikube as production proof, skipped minor upgrades, kubelet-first order, drain without capacity, etcd-only recovery, or backup without restore.
    repair: Confirm support and skew, scan removed APIs, canary components and workloads, preserve keys and state, rehearse restore order, and verify user service.
content.caption: Runnable Kubernetes object and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: kubeadm installs a complete production network, storage, load balancer, backup, and observability platform. This fails the K8s Kubeadm Controlplane Etcd contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
